sky-wash.$description: Sky Wash — Page canvas — pale blue sets the airier alternative to pure white and gives the page a soft atmospheric base

paper-white.$description: Paper White — Card and elevated surface — a whisper cooler than pure white, keeps surfaces from feeling stark against the sky canvas

cloud-veil.$description: Cloud Veil — Subtle alternate surface and fill wash for muted UI elements that need separation without contrast

pure-white.$description: Pure White — Nested card surfaces, icon fills, and button text — the brightest stop in the stack

midnight-ink.$description: Midnight Ink — Primary text and heading color — near-black with a cool cast, achieves 19.5:1 against white for maximum readability

pressed-charcoal.$description: Pressed Charcoal — Primary action buttons and dark UI blocks — the only filled action color in the system, creates decisive contrast against the pale canvas

stone.$description: Stone — Body text, hairline borders, and icon strokes — the workhorse neutral for everything between headings and the canvas

fog.$description: Fog — Muted helper text, secondary borders, and disabled states — sits between Stone and the canvas to recede gracefully

cornflower.$description: Cornflower — Illustration accent and decorative icon stroke — the cool counterweight to the warm accent trio

tangerine.$description: Tangerine — Illustration accent and decorative warm pop — provides energy and contrast in character and icon design

amethyst.$description: Amethyst — Illustration accent and decorative stroke — adds the cool-purple note that distinguishes the palette from a typical blue-orange scheme

mustard.$description: Mustard — Illustration accent and decorative warm-yellow stroke — the earthy counterpoint that keeps the palette from feeling synthetic

signal-blue.$description: Signal Blue — Link text, active link borders, and decorative brand-blue fills — the chromatic voice for navigation and emphasis

deep-cobalt.$description: Deep Cobalt — Hover/active state for Signal Blue, gradient stop in the brand blue gradient, and decorative illustration stroke

morning-tint.$description: Morning Tint — Tinted card and chip background — the lightest blue stop, used for callout cards and soft highlights

lilac-mist.$description: Lilac Mist — Tinted card background for purple-coded sections or category chips

sprout.$description: Sprout — Gray wash for highlight backgrounds, decorative bands, and soft emphasis behind content

buttery-gradient.$description: Buttery Gradient — Gradient: linear-gradient(rgb(255, 249, 224) 0%, rgb(255, 236, 163) 100%) — soft warm wash for feature callouts

lilac-gradient.$description: Lilac Gradient — Gradient: linear-gradient(rgb(244, 235, 255) 0%, rgb(228, 204, 255) 100%) — soft purple wash for feature callouts

sky-gradient.$description: Sky Gradient — Gradient: linear-gradient(rgb(229, 246, 255) 0%, rgb(194, 233, 255) 100%) — soft blue wash for feature callouts

sunset-gradient.$description: Sunset Gradient — Gradient: linear-gradient(rgb(255, 242, 235) 0%, rgb(255, 209, 184) 100%) — warm orange wash for feature callouts

brand-blue-gradient.$description: Brand Blue Gradient — Gradient: linear-gradient(rgb(71, 157, 255) 11.43%, rgb(0, 105, 224) 78.2%) — the signature brand gradient for hero accents and premium surfaces

aeonik.$description: Display and heading family — a single weight (500) across all display sizes from 20px to 148px, with tight -0.02em tracking that tightens the wordmark without becoming geometric. The "case" feature standardizes letterforms for brand consistency. The signature 148px hero size paired with 1.05 line-height creates a poster-like headline block.

geist.$description: Body, UI, caption, and button family — Geist handles every functional text from 10px micro-labels to 20px subheads. Weight 500 is the default, 600 reserved for emphasis. The -0.01em tracking and "case" feature keep utility text aligned with the display family.

sky-canvas.$description: Surface level 0: Page background, sets the airy pastel atmosphere

paper-card.$description: Surface level 1: Default card and panel surface, lifts content off the canvas

pure-white-nest.$description: Surface level 2: Nested elements, icons inside cards, and button text

tinted-chip.$description: Surface level 3: Category-tinted cards and soft highlight washes

veil.$description: Surface level 4: Muted fill surface for inactive or secondary blocks
